entry.title: Format and Display JSON in Your App
entry.content:
I wouldn't have thought this would be a big deal, but I recently got a call from a colleague in Ireland who was building an app for an important customer. And the customer had a requirement to retrieve data and display it in formatted JSON.

So my colleague asked: How do you you display formatted JSON?

At first, we both thought you could simply use the ENCODE_JSON formula function and then display in a Text field, but that did not work. But then I discovered (with the help of a friend 😽) that that function has a parameter, and that is all you need.

How to Do It

To demonstrate, I connected to the Northwind Product entity.

I then created a data variable that returned the first product, again, just to have some data.

On my UI, I created a container with a text box, whose content was set to a page variable, textToDisplay (with an initial value). I used the built-in style Primary Card to make it look nice.

SIDE NOTE: I used the custom width/height setting to make the container a minimum size but let it expand with the data. 

I then added a Set Page Variable in the data variable logic to set the page variable.

And I set the page variable to the formula below. Note some interesting things:

  • If you set the Spaces parameter of ENCODE_JSON, you will get the JSON nicely formatted.
  • Again, notice the formula editor has really good documentation on every function (if I would only read it 🤦).
ENCODE_JSON(data.Products1,4)

The result?

Without the Spaces parameter, it would have displayed like this:

Making It Editable

You could also put the text in an input box so a user could edit it.

Then you could provide a button to validate the JSON they type by using a simple JavaScript flow function.

I set the input to the page variable, changed the output to true/false, and used the following code:

If I click Validate with the default JSON:

If I make an error:

👉 Let me know if this was helpful, and maybe how you would improve on this.

entry.title: SAP BTP SDK for Android 25.4.0 is now available
entry.content:
Release announcement: SAP BTP SDK for Android 25.4.0 can now be downloaded

 

 

The new version of SAP BTP SDK for Android – version 25.4.0 is now available.

 

You can download the latest version of the SDK either from the Software Center or the downloads section on develeopers.sap.com

The SAP BTP SDK for Android team brings some exciting new features to you with this new release.

In this blog, we will highlight some of the cool features that come with the new upgrade. To get a detailed overview of all the features released, please visit our user documentation and our API reference documentation.

 

Highlights

 

Wizard & Version Info

SAP BTP SDK for Android 25.4 requires Android Studio 2024.3.1 or later.

Additionally, the Wizard now fully supports Complex OData Types in the scaffolding applications it generates. Whether you're using the generated app as a reference or a starting point, it will handle OData services with complex types, simplifying the development of UIs based on complex data structures.

 

SAP Fiori

As with every release, version 25.4 brings exciting enhancements to the Fiori UI Libraries.

The Jetpack Compose library has new and enhanced components, including support for laser scanners, enhanced Rating Controls, and a redesigned Collection View. The Date Picker now integrates with the Fiori Compose Calendar, offering features like year selection, date range selection, and the ability to clear selected dates. Additionally, AI Progress Indicators and Banner control updates bring real-time feedback and support for multiple error messages, improving the user experience.

For View-Based components, the Inline Signature Capture now includes mandatory field indicators, while the Object Cell component supports multiline text for more flexible content display. The Banner component can now display multiple error messages, and a new inline notice component has been introduced to inform users when content is AI-generated, promoting transparency and trust.

 

Foundation

In SAP BTP SDK for Android 25.4, significant updates have been made. A key enhancement is the introduction of a digits-only passcode policy, which allows admins to enforce numeric-only passcodes, along with displaying a digits-only keypad to streamline user interactions. Additionally, a new passcode history policy can be configured by the admins to define how many previous passcodes a user cannot reuse when resetting their passcode. The SDK also sees an upgrade to the Baidu library, now at version 8.3.68.0, ensuring continued support for location-based services and map-related functionality.

 

OData

The Offline OData Library has also been enhanced in this release. It now supports Crossjoin Data Aggregation, making it easier to perform complex queries in offline scenarios. Furthermore, the UNBIND operation has been added to the UpdateEntity requests, providing more flexibility when managing entity relationships offline.

 

Raise A Request

If you have feature ideas that the SDK should support, please raise an improvement request here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/2279.

 

Feature Summary

This blog highlights some of the features released with the new version of SAP BTP SDK for Android – 25.4.0. For details, please check the important links section.

  • Support for digits-only passcodes and enhanced admin-enforced passcode history policies
  • New Fiori controls such as AI Notice and AI Progress Indicators, along with enhancements to other controls such as Date Picker and Banner
  • Baidu library upgrade to version 8.3.68.0
  • Support for crossjoin data aggregation and the UNBIND operation in Offline OData

 

Important Links

  • SAP BTP SDK for Android 25.4.0 (Software Center)
  • Documentation
  • API Reference

 

Acknowledgement

A huge kudos to the spectacular team that worked tirelessly, right from conceptualizing the improvements, to defining, designing, building, and eventually delivering them.

entry.title: SAP BTP SDK for iOS 25.4.0 is now available
entry.content:
Release announcement: SAP BTP SDK for iOS 25.4.0 can be downloaded

 

The new version of SAP BTP SDK for iOS 25.4.0 is now generally available.

 

You can download the latest version of the SDK either from the Software Center or the downloads section on develeopers.sap.com

With this latest release, the SAP BTP SDK for iOS team has brought some exciting new features for you to explore.

In this blog, we will highlight the new upgrade's offerings. Please visit our documentation and API reference to get a detailed overview of all the features released.

 

Highlights

 

Version Information

With version 25.4, the SAP BTP SDK for iOS supports iOS 17 and higher versions and requires Xcode 16.2 or newer for app development.

 

SAP Fiori

As with every release, version 25.4 brings a number of important Fiori enhancements. The new AI Notice component provides a mechanism for developers to transparently indicate when AI-generated content is presented, helping to build user trust. Additionally, the new AI Progress Indicator provides an icon and a descriptive message to offer users clear context during AI-driven processes. Toast messages have been enhanced to deliver a smoother and more consistent notification experience. The FUIAttachmentViewController now clearly indicates which attachments are mandatory and can be configured to display a confirmation dialog when users attempt to delete attachments.

 

Foundation Enhancements

On the foundation side, developers will find new capabilities that improve diagnostics and strengthen user security. Applications can now export local client logs without requiring user authentication, making it easier to troubleshoot issues before onboarding is complete. Additionally, administrators can now enforce a digits-only passcode policy, which streamlines security compliance and enhances the corporate user experience by automatically displaying a digits-only keyboard.

 

OData

Offline functionality continues to be a strong focus area. In this release, Offline OData has been enhanced to support crossjoin data aggregation, making it easier to perform complex offline queries. Additionally, support for the UNBIND operation in UpdateEntity requests has been added, allowing developers greater flexibility when managing entity relationships in offline scenarios.

 

Raise A Request

If you have feature ideas that the SDK should support, please raise an improvement request here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/2279.

 

Feature Summary

This blog highlights some of the key features introduced in SAP BTP SDK for iOS 25.4.0. For more detailed information, please refer to the important links section below.

  • Support for Xcode 16.2 and iOS 17+
  • Digits-only passcode support
  • New Fiori Controls like AI Notice and Progress Indicator
  • Crossjoin Data Aggregation and UNBIND operation in UpdateEntity

 

Important Links

  • SAP BTP SDK for iOS 25.4 Download (Software Center)
  • Documentation
  • API Reference

 

Acknowledgement

A huge kudos to the spectacular team that worked tirelessly, right from conceptualizing the improvements, to defining, designing, building, and eventually delivering them.

entry.title: Introducing the New Pricing and Packaging for SAP Build: A Unified Experience for all Developers
entry.content:
As SAP is evolving its Business Suite, AI, and Data Products to meet rising market demands, and as part of this strategy, we are introducing a new exciting, unified commercial model for SAP Build. This updated offering consolidates capabilities for pro-code, low code and AI powered development tools making it easier than ever to build applications and extensions within the SAP ecosystem.

With built-in access to runtime, automation, and extensibility services, SAP Build now empowers both business users and professional developers to choose the right tool set for the right job. The integration of generative AI enhances productivity by streamlining workflows, automating tasks, and accelerating decision-making.

To further support development, SAP Build capabilities along with SAP HANA Cloud are now fully included in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Packages empowering customers to handle the majority of their expected scenarios right out of the box. Read the full announcement here.

What changes?

We've heard our customers loud and clear; they asked for simpler licensing, easier provisioning, and a unified toolset. Previously, each SAP Build product like Build Apps, Process Automation, Work Zone, Build Code, and ABAP was licensed separately. This resulted in having lots of different services for customers to manage.  

The following list of 14 services from SAP Build have now been consolidated:

SAP Build Services:

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
  • SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Task Center
  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Mobile Services
  • Application Frontend Service
  • SAP Business Application Studio

 SAP Build Runtime Services:

  • SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Runtime
  • SAP BTP ABAP environment
  • SAP BTP, Kyma runtime
  • SAP Document Management Service
  • SAP Cloud Transport Management
  • SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery

 Now you can access the full range of SAP Build capabilities listed above through just four distinct licenses:

SAP Build base user

This edition is for users who need to access applications, gain insights for day-to-day business needs, and approve tasks. SAP Build base user includes:

  • Access to digital workspaces
  • Access web and mobile apps
  • Request and approve workflow tasks
  • Access mobile start
  • 1000 API Calls
  • 100 MB Storage

 Note: Prerequisite is at least one Build Developer license to manage SAP Build.

 SAP Build premium user

This edition is for users who author content, monitor processes, customize pages, and deploy bots. SAP Build premium user includes:

  • Everything in SAP Build base user, plus
  • Monitor processes via the dashboard
  • Deploy attended robotic process automation bots
  • Author and access workspace content
  • Visually design digital workspaces and guided experiences
  • 1000 API Calls
  • 100 MB Storage

 Note: Prerequisite is at least one Build Developer license to manage SAP Build.

SAP Build developer

This edition is for users who develop application/extensions, workflows, and automations for S/4 and other LoB apps. SAP Build developer includes:

  • Everything in SAP Build premium user, plus
  • Build with low-code/no-code developer tools
  • Develop with code-first developer tools (IDEs)
  • Create and deploy apps and extensions
  • Develop and deploy mobile applications
  • Design and deploy workflows
  • 5000 API Calls
  • 500 MB Storage

SAP Build runtime

Required for application-hosting and lifecycle management services that support the creation and execution for dev/test as well as production environments. SAP Build runtime includes:

  • SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime
  • SAP BTP ABAP Environment
  • SAP BTP, Kyma runtime
  • Application runtime
  • Document management storage and API
  • Cloud transport management
  • Continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD)

Note: Customers can access applications hosted on runtimes without using any end-user capabilities. This ensures customers aren't charged for user licenses they don’t actively use.

Find detailed information about SAP Build and what’s in scope on the SAP Help Portal. 

What are the key benefits of the new SAP Build packaging, and how will it help our customers?

The new SAP Build packaging delivers a streamlined, powerful experience for building extensions, automations, and apps across the SAP ecosystem. Here's how it benefits customers:

  • Unified Access Across Tools: SAP Build now brings together low-code, pro-code, and AI-powered development tools under a single license removing the complexity of managing multiple products and licenses.
  • Built-In ERP Integration: SAP Build and SAP HANA Cloud entitlements are now included in SAP Cloud ERP Private Packages, enabling faster innovation and development for SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers.
  • Empowerment for Developers & Business Users: Both professional developers and key users gain the flexibility to personalize SAP systems with greater speed up to 3x faster, according to
  • Optimized Cost & Efficiency: A flexible, usage-based runtime model helps reduce total cost of ownership and streamline operational overhead.
  • Simplified Governance: A unified licensing approach improves compliance management and centralizes governance across all tools.
  • Business Value First: By removing commercial and technical barriers, organizations can focus more on driving transformation and delivering real business outcomes.

With SAP Build, customers can:

  • Scale confidently within the SAP ecosystem: Use included licenses to rapidly build compliant extensions that align with SAP standards and scale across the enterprise.
  • Leverage AI to boost productivity: Automate complex cross-system processes with AI agents, customize Joule with skill-specific tasks, and speed up development using SAP-optimized AI tools.
  • Deliver faster from front-end to full-stack: Build complete extensions with prebuilt templates, low-code tools, and integrated services like authentication, deployment, and lifecycle management—all on a single, seamless platform.

 Commercial Model and Availability

As of 22nd April 2025, SAP Build is available to customers for subscription only. Availability for consumption under BTPEA and PayGo is anticipated by the end of Q2.

Call to action

  • Visit the SAP Build pricing page to learn more
  • Help portal documentation
  • Register for the SAP Build webinar on May 8th
  • Read the full announcement here
  • Visit the SAP Discovery Center for more details.
  • Review pricing on Discovery Center

entry.title: Partners unlock innovation with latest SAP Build capabilities
entry.content:
20 Partners from 10 countries participated and built innovative solutions during the recently concluded Hack2Build on SAP Build. With the latest SAP Build capabilities delivered recently by SAP, Partners explored and leveraged key functionalities of the low-code, pro-code and generative AI tools for building their innovations.

Partners extend, create, and automate with SAP Build

In this Hack2Build edition, partner used SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Work Zone, and SAP Build Process Automation integration and built applications to guide users through a multi-step process and efficient completion of complex business processes. Partners also used SAP Build Code, the AI-powered code assistance for application creation and code optimization.

The hack phase started on April 8,2025, and concluded on April 15, 2025, with two demo jams. The demo jam saw the partners present their innovations developed using SAP Build in just five working days. A panel of judges chose three winners each, impressed by high-powered pitches.

Hack2Build SAP Build-Partners who participated in the final demo jamIn this blog, we will highlight the three winners from each of the two groups who showcased their innovations developed on SAP Build.

Group 1 Winners

Hack2Build SAP Build - Group1 winners

 Jupiter 2000 showcased a Building Facade Inspection Safety Program (FISP) Application enhancing building inspections with AI-generated suggestions and with guided experience report submission. The guided experience offers inspectors to fill out reports accurately and in compliance with regulations. Using SAP AI Core, AI-Powered photo identification for crack detection and image recognition to generate automatically description of facade defects with manual override as needed. The application allows for reviewing, editing, and submitting inspection reports before final approval with a possibility of submission to government departments for streamlined reporting.

Jupiter 2000's Façade Inspection Safety Program (FISP) Application

Reply introduced NextGen Service Portal designed to improve service request reporting by providing a guided, web-based experience for reporting service tickets using SAP Work Zone and SAP Build Apps. It is a pre-configured guided solution for service-intensive industries which allows internal employees, external partners, and even unknown users to report issues through a simple visual and structured web-based experience. With a guided step-by-step process to report service tickets, structured data input, capability of interactive floor maps and upload of photos, the data can flow directly to SAP Service Cloud for real-time ticket management.

NextGen Service Portal by Reply

EY developed Smart Automation of Goods Receipts and Purchase Order creation with a central SAP Work Zone site and SAP Build Apps for purchase order (PO) creation, and goods receipt QR Code scanning which eliminates the need for third-party scanner tools, utilizing AI for planning suggestions. The solution eliminates the need for third-party scanner tools, offers a Clean Core compliant and AI-integrated process, with automated and Instant creation of Goods Receipts (GR) or Goods Issues (GI) in SAP S/4HANA, enhancing efficiency and flexibility for warehouse staff using mobile devices.

EY's Smart Automation of Goods Receipts and Purchase Order creation

Group 2 Winners

Hack2Build SAP Build - Group2 winners

MOURI Tech developed Guide2Resolve, a complaint resolution solution to streamline complaints at large events like expos and provide enhanced attendee experience and improved organizational efficiency. Guide2Resolve simplifies and accelerates the complaint submission and resolution process using a guided Process, structured, and automated flow powered by SAP Build Work Zone and UI Integration Cards. Generative AI determines category based on issue description.

Guide2Resolve application by MOURI Tech

Cognizant showcased their Case management AI Agent for SAP. They offer a side by side extensibility solution that adheres to SAP clean core principles designed to automate processes, provides user guidance, and improves efficiency, particularly notable in the utilities sector. The solution brings a unified view of all exceptions, and offers case resolution using guided procedures, utilizes SAP Build, Work Zone, and Process Automation to create an efficient digital workspace. The solution harnesses the power of generative AI to deliver a suite of multi-agents that transform case management.

Case Management AI Agent for SAP by Cognizant

NTT Data’s agri business solution seeks to improve the integration of large-scale weighing operations, crucial for grain transportation. The application receive weights and quality analysis upon soft commodity movements, indicate which trading contract should be used, allows automatic or manual application to contracts, executes goods movement and issues invoicing documents. The solution developed with SAP Build Work Zone with the support of Joule during the development process, utilizes SAP CAP with SAP HANA HDI Containers with a custom SAPUI5 front end. The solution connects to SAP S/4HANA applications by the SAP Cloud Connector.

 NTT Agro – Scale and application

Judges 

Many thanks to the panel of judges who spent considerable time to evaluate and share feedback on the innovative solutions presented by the partners.

Pavel PenazGlobal Vice President & Head of Partner Solution Success BTP & AI, SAP Czech Republic
Kathrin HettelVice President, Strategic Cross-Portfolio & AI Partnership, SAP SE
Julia IvanovaSAP Build Product Management, SAP SE
Marc HuberSAP Build Product Management, SAP SE
Sruthi ChiramelSAP Build Product Management, SAP SE
Stephan KoenigSAP Build Product Management, SAP SE
Neeraj UpretiSAP Ecosystem Development Manager, SAP America

Special Thanks

We extend our appreciation to the SAP team members from SAP Build Product Management and Partner Solution Success, who coached the partners to successfully develop their solutions during the Hack2Build SAP Build initiative.

Next Phase

Partners have expressed their interest to complete their full scope solution in 10 weeks. Partner Solution Success will engage with all the participating partners to support their build journey in the next weeks and get their innovations ready for customer adoption.

entry.title: Automating Meeting Insights into SAP BPA
entry.content:
How the extension works today:

1 - A SAP BPA flow triggers a small Python script.

2 - The script ingests a Word document transcript of the meeting.

3 - It sends the text to the OpenAI API, which returns a structured JSON with detected intents and action items.

4 - BPA bot reads the JSON and sends follow-up e-mails that execute specific requests voiced during the meeting — e.g., "Please check the status of ticket 90010," "Request an updated ETA from the printer-services vendor," etc. (not recap e-mails, which SAP Joule already covers). Future iterations will also create SAP objects such as tasks, quotations , purchase requisitions directl and etc.

Expected value:
Once operational, the assistant could save several hours of manual coordination per meeting, especially in management sessions where follow-up actions tend to be numerous and cross-functional.

Next milestone:
I plan to evolve the add-on into a real-time companion to SAP Joule by:

Capturing live audio transcripts instead of relying on Word uploads;

Enriching context with CRM/BTP data before inference;

Feeding the final JSON back into SAP Build for straight-through processing across multiple languages.

Combined with SAP Joule, this would complete the full loop: listen → understand → act → monitor.

I’d appreciate your thoughts on the architecture, technology and positioning before moving to a wider pilot. At the moment I think SAP Joule coud send events after meetings, or executions to BPA, so the bot will be able  to be trigerred.

What I need from you

I’m looking for feedback on the project’s overall architecture and guidance on the best SAP BTP technologies to use.
SAP has announced that Joule will soon integrate with Copilot, so I’d also like your view on whether we should eventually replace GPT with Copilot.
In short, is the idea solid, what existing BTP services or components can we leverage today, and how should we evolve the solution for the future?

Lets create Julio hahahah the Husband of Joule.


Workflow:

For full explanation watch te following video:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oyvg3O-acaw

entry.title: Add Value Help with SAP Build Actions (free project)
entry.content:
Value help is one of the key features that can make a UI really friendly. Here's how to do it without much work, using the new integration of Actions with SAP Build Apps.

Even if you do not need value help, you can learn from this project:

  • Actions (including custom $filter)
  • 🆕 New page variables based on existing schema feature
  • Custom events
  • Popups (including showing/hiding and absolute positioning)
  • Layouting and positioning 
  • Events related to input box
  • 🆕 Integrating actions into SAP Build Apps 
  • Working with destinations to retrieve S/4HANA data
  • Paging and filtering

As always we will use the A_BusinessPartner entity so the user can easily select a business partner. The app has 2 ways to provide the value help:

🟪From within an Input Box:

🟪From another screen that lists all the business partners, using paging and filtering:

NOTE: This technique is good for an OData v2 service. You can do the same for a v4 service, though there you could use the CONTAINS keyword in the action or even skip the action and use a "Get record collection" to do the filtering work, since for v4 services this flow function provides the contains filtering option.

Much of the idea for this UI is based on a blog by @botazhat and her blog How to add a search bar with suggestions in SAP Build Apps. I have enhanced it by using actions, and making it work with v2 services and taking data directly from SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

 

Create Action for Getting Help Values

I think it is better design to use an action to get the help values since it.

  • Hides the API calls from the app developer
  • Hides the filtering syntax, which can be complex and different between OData v2 and v4
  • Makes the front-end development simpler

You can download the project, though it is quite simple to create yourself.

All you have to do to get started is create an action based on the BusinessPartner API service, and use the simple GET all business partners call from the A_BusinessPartner entity.

You will make a few small changes to make it easier to work with:

  • Add filterString as an input parameter to get a string to search in the business partner name.
  • Set defaults for some of the OData query parameters:
    • Set $top to 20, to retrieve only 20 matches at once (this can be overridden by the app)
    • Set $orderby to sort by BusinessPartnerFullName is ascending order (but you can change this)
    • Set $inlinecount to allpages, to return the total number of records, for the case where we paginate through the values.

For the filtering, we set the $filter input. Click the row, which opens the side panel. Then in the side panel, click to open the expression editor.

This will let you you set the $filter clause, either with a GUI or manually by typing in whatever you want.

This is the expression I used, to search for all matches on the BusinessPartnerFullName field using the value in filterString, and eliminating all business partners with no name. Note in v4 we would have had simpler syntax with the CONTAINS keyword.

substringof('${filterString}',BusinessPartnerFullName) eq true and BusinessPartnerFullName ne ''

Release and publish the action.

 

The Project

You can download the project we used to see how the UI, variables and logic. To use it, you'll have to connect your own deployed action. I've tried to make it as generic as possible.

There 2 pages, each providing a different way to get value help:

  • Home Page: Contains the form for business partner ID and name, plus the dropdown to search for a specific business partner.
  • Business Partners: Contains a list of all business partners that you can paginate through or search through.

And we have 2 app variables to store the business partner ID and name.

UI for Dropdown Help

For both pages, we create an integration to the action, and to the destination to be used with the action. Notice that here we create an integration to a destination without enabling any entities -- this was a little weird to me the first time I saw it. 

For variables, we have these:

  • Action_In and Action_Out are created automatically with the new feature that allows you to create variables from an action's schema.
  • filterString is the search string.
  • events is a way to control the updates when someone types fast and reduce the number of calls to the backend (write a comment if you want to learn more). 

And the logic is pretty simple with only 3 main flow functions (skipping all the flow functions related to the events variable):

  1. We capture when someone types in the search field (EVENT)
  2. We ignore when the search string is less than 3 characters (IF CONDITION)
  3. We then run the action (TRIGGER ACTION)
  4. We set the variable to hold the returned values (SET PAGE VARIABLE)

We also added some simple logic to handle when the user leaves the search box or presses escape (clear the value help) or when the user returns (run the original logic above with custom event). 

As for the UI, the main thing is the container holding the value help is absolutely positioned so it aligns with the input box:

And its visibility is set only when there are options to display:

UI for Selecting from All Values

Instead of using a dropdown, we added an icon at the end of the input box that navigates to a different page for selecting the business partner. 

This takes us to a separate page where we display all the business partners, and we can use filtering or paging to find the record we want.

This is the only logic we have, on the page logic canvas (except for clicking one of the business partners and returning to the home page).

When we come to the page we load all the business partners, and we have a custom event to refresh the page whenever we need to.

Then we have 3 flows when different page variables change:

  • User changes search string: We reset the page number to 1 and the OData $skip parameter to 0, and call the backend again.
  • User navigates to a different page: We recalculate the OData $skip parameter, and call the backend again.
  • System changes the # of retrieved records: We recalculate the number of pages we have. This occurs basically whenever we call the backend.

 

Room for Improvement

There are small things we could have done to make it even better:

  • Put the icon for the full business partner list inside the input box, making it a custom component (something Bota did in her blog)
  • Add multi-selection and wildcard characters
  • Package it to make it more generic for any data entity

Also, I wanted to eliminate flashing when the user enters a search string, and there was a tradeoff between the flashing and the speed. I might change my calculus to make it faster.

I'll work on these. In the meantime, tell me what you thought of these techniques, including how you would improve them.

😸

  • Simplify SAPUI5 Code Extensions with the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard (blog post) https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/simplify-sapui5-code-extensions-with-the-sap-build-extensibility-wizard/ba-p/14087839

SAP Build New Pricing and Packaging

  • Introducing the New Pricing and Packaging for SAP Build: A Unified Experience for all Developers (blog post) https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/introducing-the-new-pricing-and-packaging-for-sap-build-a-unified/ba-p/14086382

 

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 ABAP Challenge for May

1:00 UI5ers live

1:48 New adapters in SAP Integration Suite

2:38 SAP Build Extensibility Wizard

3:46 SAP Build New Pricing and Packaging 

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for May the 1st, 2025.

[Mamikee] We have an exciting new developer challenge for the month of May. And yes, it's all about ABAP. This time, we're putting the spotlight on extensibility. That means we'll be getting into areas like adjusting software behavior beyond standard configuration, extending the data model, and exposing additional data, and much more. Now, the ABAP room will be unlocked starting May 5th and will remain open until the end of the year. But remember, the challenge closes on May 31st, so make sure to escape the room before that to earn your badge. Now, just like previous challenges, participating is really easy. All you need to do is visit the SAP Community Escape House, enter your numeric SAP Community user ID, and then you select the ABAP Limit Room. To learn more about the challenge and many more others, click the link in the description below. And remember, have fun!

[Nico] Hi everyone and welcome to the sap developer news we would like to point your attention to the upcoming ui5ers live webinar happening on May 8th and it will be about AI-powered ui5 applications built with LM studio and the speaker of the webinar will be Peter Music the Chief development architect of ui5 and I definitely think you shouldn't miss this one it's open it's free and anybody can join. And in case you're interested in the previous 44 episodes of UI5ers Live, you can watch the recordings of those on the UI5 YouTube channel. I'll put the link to that in the description of this video as well. All right, hope to talk to you soon. Bye.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. In the integration space, this week is all about new adapters available in SAP Integration Suite. There's a new adapter for Anaplan and HubSpot. Interacting with databases there's a new adapter for Azure Cosmos DB. To power your event-driven architectures, the following new adapters are available. Amazon EventBridge, Google PubSub, IBM MQ REST adapter, and Salesforce PubSub adapter. Related to enterprise architecture management, there's a new LeanIX adapter. And last but centrally, not least, the RFC adapter is coming for the Edge Integrational Cell Runtime. If you want to learn more about each adapter, check out the links, including the description. Ciao!

[DJ] There are a lot of different approaches for extending SAP Fiori apps, powered by SAPUI5, of course. There are adaptation projects for key users and for developers, and there's SAPUI5 flexibility. There's a helpful blog post by Oliver Gref that covers these approaches and also mentions the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard that you should definitely check out. The wizard was introduced in the context of SAP S4 HANA Cloud and consolidates multiple extension options. The most recent update added the ability to create sap ui5 adaptation projects making it the go-to facility in sap build for extending sap business suite check out Oliver's blog post it's got lots of really helpful links to the right parts of the SAP help portal on all of this link in the description

[Thomas] Hey, I'm here in the Newtown Square offices with Josh Bentley, and we're working on a workshop around our organization, but I want to talk to you today about SAP Build. Just Announce is new pricing and bundling options for SAP Build. So in the past, if you wanted to use multiple of the capabilities that are part of SAP Build, You had to license and enable them separately, and there's quite a few of those capabilities. Maybe you want to use build code and build process automation and build apps, and that was a lot of work. The powers that be at SAP heard your feedback around that, and they want to make it easier for you to use all the capabilities of SAP Build. Therefore, there's new bundling options where you could purchase SAP Build and a single license that will give you all the capabilities under SAP Build, including the ABAP cloud capabilities. So to read more about all the options, how they're priced, how they're sized, check out Thomas Balmering's blog post. The link to that will be in the description below.

entry.title: The Next Generation of Business Leaders Must Master This…
entry.content:
The Next Generation of Business Leaders Must Master This…


Enterprise leaders today are navigating an era of rapid technological change. AI, automation, and cloud-based platforms are transforming industries, but many organizations are struggling to keep up. The leaders of the future must think differently, act boldly, and leverage technology strategically to drive business success.

What will define the next generation of enterprise leaders? Here are the key areas of mastery:

 1. Think Like a CEO, Act Like a Technologist
Leadership today is no longer about simply managing people—it’s about managing technology-driven transformation. The best leaders will:

✅Understand the power of AI and automation – Tools like SAP Joule bring AI-driven insights directly into business strategy.
✅Leverage real-time data – SAP Business Data Cloud enables leaders to make decisions based on live analytics rather than outdated reports.
✅Foster a culture of innovation – Driving technology adoption across business units ensures agility and resilience.

 2. Master AI, Automation, and Low-Code
Future leaders must not only understand business strategy but also how technology fuels it. The ability to:

🔹Deploy AI-driven automation through platforms like SAP Build Process Automation is critical.
🔹Optimize workflows with AI-powered analytics from SAP Business AI to drive efficiency.
🔹Enable citizen development through SAP Build so that business users can create their own digital solutions.

 3. Embrace Change as the New Constant
The business world is evolving faster than ever. Future leaders must:

🛠Be comfortable with continuous digital transformation.
📊Use data-driven insights to pivot strategies in real time.
⚙️Drive cross-functional collaboration between IT and business units to break down silos.

 The Future of Enterprise Leadership

To stay ahead, tomorrow’s leaders must:

✅Combine strategic vision with technological expertise.
✅Invest in AI, automation, and data-driven decision-making.
✅Create an adaptable, agile workforce ready for the digital future.

The leaders who embrace technology, data, and automation will be the ones driving the future of business.

What skills do you think the next generation of enterprise leaders need most?

entry.title: SAP Sapphire Orlando 2025:Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP that you can implement today
entry.content:
Hello, SAP Community!

My name is Joanne Koh-Stewart, and I am Director of Product Marketing for SAP BTP for Business Applications and Core. I focus on identifying and nurturing SAP BTP use cases that solve real customer challenges across the Business Suite and lines of business. You might have seen my name around Sapphire and TechEd times, writing about our SAP BTP use cases.

I'm pleased to announce that I'll be presenting again at this year's SAP Sapphire Orlando. My session Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP that you can implement today with my colleague @denisema, is scheduled for Tuesday, May 20th at 11:30am – 11:50am EDT.

 

What you will learn about:

This session is particularly exciting because it allows me to share insights gained from our customers and partners who leveraged SAP BTP. The idea for this session emerged from numerous conversations with customers eager to understand what they could do with SAP BTP and how they could quickly implement it to address their pressing business challenges. We've curated a selection of use cases that demonstrate tangible value and can be rapidly deployed.  

For customers who want to try out SAP BTP but are not ready to commit, this session will dive into how customers have different options to get started for free with SAP BTP.

 

We look forward to seeing you:

Whether you're well-versed in SAP BTP or just beginning to explore its potential, this session promises valuable takeaways on business challenges that SAP BTP can help resolve. Denise and I look forward to meeting you at the session or at the BTP for Business Application booth at SAP Sapphire Orlando, and engaging in insightful discussions about the transformative power of SAP BTP and how it can address your specific business needs. Make sure to add Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP that you can implement today session to your Favourites to add it to your Sapphire agenda. 

 

Other related sessions I recommend:  

Mission possible: Accelerate transformation with SAP Discovery Center - BTP2467 

Related community managed tags you should subscribe to remain informed about this topic: 

  • SAP Business Technology Platform
  • SAP Build
  • SAP Business Suite
  • SAP Integration Suite

Related community topic pages: 

  •  SAP Business Technology Platform In Action

 

Now let's hear from you:

  • How are you currently driving digital transformation within your organization?
  • What are your main goals for using SAP BTP?
  • What are/were your biggest challenges in getting started with SAP BTP?

Tell us in the comments, and we look forward to seeing you virtual and/or in Orlando in a few weeks!

entry.title: Kickstart Your Developer Journey – Get SAP-Certified in Low-Code/No-Code
entry.content:
New to development? No problem! SAP offers a certification and learning resources that help you build real business apps, workflows, and user experiences — without needing to be a coding expert. Using tools like SAP Build Apps, Work Zone, and Process Automation, you’ll be surprised how much you can do just by dragging, dropping, and configuring.

 

Simple Steps to Get Certified

 

Step 1: Learn at Your Own Pace Follow these beginner-friendly learning paths:

  • SAP Build Apps: Build apps visually without needing to write code. Learn how to connect data, create user-friendly pages, and define app behavior.
  • SAP Build Work Zone: Set up a digital workspace where users can access their apps and content in one place.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: Learn how to build simple workflows and automation tools to save time and reduce manual work.

Step 2: Practice What You Learn You’ll try a real-world simulation using everything you’ve learned. This hands-on practice is required before taking the certification exam and helps boost your confidence.

Step 3: Take the Certification Exam Once you feel ready, book your exam. Passing it proves your skills and gives you an official SAP Certification — a great addition to your resume!

 

Want to Keep Growing?

 

  • Watch tutorials on YouTube from SAP Developer Advocates.
  • Join local SAP CodeJam events to meet and learn from other developers.
  • Subscribe to SAP Learning Hub for more guided learning from SAP experts.

 

Whether you're starting your career or switching to tech, this certification is a great way to get hands-on with real business tools — and build your future.

entry.title: SAP Developer News May 8th, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

Mass Import Excel to SAP CAP – Now Made Simple with CDS Plugin 

  • Blog post https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/mass-import-excel-sap-cap-now-made-simple-cds-plugin-marian-zeis-midfc/  
  • The Hitchhiker’s Guide To The Galaxy reference https://www.clivebanks.co.uk/THHGTTG/THHGTTGradio2.htm#:~:text=without%20all%20that%20tedious%20mucking%20about%20in%20hyperspace

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for May 8th, 2025.

[DJ] So the community is still excelling in collaboration and producing top quality code and content. Here is a recent example from Marian Zeis. Now, it involves the UI5 spreadsheet importer, which we've covered on previous news items on this show. but this time it also involves a cds plugin for the sap cloud application programming model which means it's now possible to upload and bypass the routing of data the mass data through the front end and all the subsequent odata calls and the processing involved and upload directly to a cap back end the cds plugin was originally written by wouter lemer another friend of the community and it allows this direct import to a capped back end without all that tedious mucking about in hyperspace and front end of course that was a reference to the Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy if you didn't recognize that already with the latest release that's 2.1.0 of the UI5 spreadsheet importer you can now enable the use of this CDS plugin and upload with no code required on the front-end to change. Pretty neat. Anyway, Marian's blog post has all the details checking out. Link in the description.

[Vitaliy] Have you heard about the Knowledge Graph engine in SAP HANA Cloud? It came as the new functionality in QRC 1 this year. Now our COE in the US prepared a discovery mission for you, where you can go through a series of exercises and ready to use code to discover what KGE is, how you use the graph transformers to convert unstructured data into the triples and store them in SAP HANA Cloud, or how to turn structured data into the ontology that you can use in your AI applications. Check this out. I just finished this discovery mission and I learned quite a few things myself. There are some other AI capabilities that were delivered during a recent couple of months and to be able to give you some more exercises, I created the new Code Jam topic that is called AI Application Fundamentals with SAP HANA Cloud. we are planning to have such a code gem as well after the hana tech con so if you are attending this event in WalIdorf in Germany in July then please check this event and decide if you want to join this sap code jam where we are going to go through all this new exciting AI functionality to you in SAP HANA, see you there.

[Michelle] Hey, y'all. For all you front-end nerds out there, the May 2025 edition of the SAP Fiori Development Newsletter is now live, and it's packed with insights you don't want to miss. This issue covers cutting-edge updates to SAPUI5, including new tools and features to streamline your development process. There's all kinds of different updates over a variety of different topics, with links going into further detail, so there's a little something for everyone. One of my big bucket list items is visiting all seven continents, and a bit of news that I found really interesting in this issue is that it turns out SAPUI5 has already beat me to it. It's on all seven continents, including Antarctica, and it's crazy to think about how truly global SAPUI5 is. If that interested you as well, and you want to learn more, or you just want to stay ahead of the curve and discover how SAP Fiori is shaping the future of development, head over to the SAP Community page to check it out now. The issue is linked below, and as always, happy coding!

[DJ] So before we end this news episode, we just wanted to make you aware of the SAP BTP Guidance Framework. You might already know about the SAP BTP Developers Guide. Well, think of the Guidance Framework as sort of one level up from that, something that contains the SAP BTP developers guide and lots more. Now the aim of this framework is to provide architects and administrators as well as developers all they need to know to build and run and maintain enterprise grade and enterprise scale applications and solutions on SAP BTP. We've included a link to the sort of landing page to the guidance framework which is on the SAP help portal and from there you have links to all the individual components. Happy learning!

entry.title: Make use of Generative AI to accelerate development in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
SAP Build Process Automation is SAP’s centralized development environment for intelligent automation and process extensibility. It has already been implemented by hundreds of customers to increase overall process efficiency and free employees from mundane and repetitive tasks.

A key driver of its success is the ease of use of the development environment and the low entry barrier for different stakeholders who want to participate in process development projects. This is largely due to an intuitive graphical layout designer, a simplified commercial model that allows you to start small and fast, and pre-built automation content available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Now, process automation becomes even simpler with the native integration of generative AI within the SAP Build Process Automation environment.

You can use generative AI in SAP Build Process Automation to generate business processes, decisions, forms, and script tasks. The goal of this blog post is to guide you on your journey to explore this feature and share some best practices.

 

System setup

To use the generative AI features, you must first enable generative AI at the tenant level and accept the terms and conditions, by navigating to the Tenant Configuration within the Control Tower:

 

 

Once enabled, the Generate function will become available after approximately 5 minutes, both in the Overview screen of the project and within the process editor:

 

 

Use-Case

In the next section, we’ll explore the key capabilities of SAP Build Process Automation that leverage Generative AI. To provide meaningful context, we’ll walk through a real-world scenario: imagine you're a senior automation developer aiming to create new process artifacts - such as Business Rules, Conditions, Process Flows, and Approval Steps. Your goal is to use natural language prompts to generate these components efficiently. We’ll demonstrate how Generative AI can accelerate this process and show you how to craft effective prompts to get the results you want. Below are some practical examples for following artifacts:

 

  • Generate a Data Type
  • Generate a Process
  • Generate a Form
  • Generate a Decision
  • Generate a Script
  • Summarize the Process

 

Generate a Data Type

We begin our automation journey by defining a Data Type. This is a crucial step, as it later enables us to handle input and output values in Conditions and Business Rules. To get started, navigate to the process overview page of your project and click on Generate:

 

 

Next, select Data Type and enter your prompt. Below are two example prompts you can use and refine as needed:

  • "Generate a data type to handle Purchasing Organizations. This data type should contain a single field named purchasingOrganizations, represented as a string."

  • "Generate a data type to handle Approvers. This data type should include two fields: approversEmail and approversName, both represented as strings."

These prompts can be adjusted iteratively to match your specific needs. As a result you now have two different Data Types which you can use as input and output values for your process steps:

 

 

Generate a Process

The next step is to generate a process flow that brings together multiple artifacts - such as Data Types, Decisions, Conditions, Forms, and more. As in the previous step, navigate to the overview page of your project and select Process. Here, you can enter a prompt to define your process logic. Below is an example you can use as a starting point:

  • "Generate a new process. Begin with a business rule to determine the approver. Then, add a condition to handle cost thresholds: if the cost exceeds 2,000 euros, trigger an approval step; if the cost is below 2,000 euros, trigger an action project to post the purchase requisition to SAP S/4HANA."

Feel free to iterate on this prompt or expand it to fit your specific process requirements.
As a result, you now have a generated process that already includes several key artifacts. From here, you can begin refining the process further or add additional components—such as user tasks, forms, notifications, or integrations - as needed to align it with your business logic and automation goals:

 

 

Generate a Form

We’ve noticed that the current process is missing a trigger—a key component to initiate the workflow. Fortunately, we can use Generative AI to create this for us. In this context, Generative AI can help generate various types of forms, including trigger forms, notification forms, and approval forms.

There’s no need to return to the overview page—you can stay within the Process Builder. Simply click on the Generate button and enter your prompt. Here’s an example:

  • "Add a trigger form to start the process. In this form, the user should enter product information, quantity, and price."

This form will serve as the entry point for the process, capturing the necessary input to drive subsequent steps.

 

 

Sometimes, the generated form may appear in an unexpected location within the process flow. Don’t worry—this is easy to fix. Simply delete the form from its current position in the flow.

To correctly assign it as the trigger:

  1. Click on Add Trigger

  2. Select Forms

  3. Choose the newly created form from the list

This will properly link the form as the starting point of your process, as you can see here:

 

 

As a post processing step I change the input text for the line-items with a table list to enter multiple values.

 

Generate a Decision

Next, we’ll incorporate a Decision into the automation flow. Decisions can be used for anything, but in our scenario, we’ll use a decision table to determine the appropriate approver based on the purchasing group.

To add a Decision:

  1. Click the plus (+) button at the point in the flow where you want to insert the artifact.

  2. Select Decision.

  3. Configure the decision by defining proper input and output values.

You can select from the Data Types we previously created to ensure your decision logic is well-integrated and functional:

 

 

To define the logic for your Decision, go to the Rules tab and click on Generate to enter your prompt. This is where you can use Generative AI to quickly create a decision table. Here’s an example prompt you can use:

  • "Create a decision table rule named 'Approver Determination' to assign approvers based on the purchasing organization. If the purchasing organization is 1710, the purchase requisition must be approved by Admin Manager 1 (manager1@sap.com). If the purchasing organization is 1720, it must be approved by Admin Manager 2 (manager2@sap.com)."

This rule will automatically evaluate input from the process and route it to the correct approver accordingly. Here you can see the final result:

 

 

Generate a Script

SAP Build Process Automation also supports the use of custom scripts within a process flow. These scripts are particularly useful for tasks such as data validation, calculations, or entity mapping. In this example, we’ll use a custom script to calculate the total price from an input table.

To do this:

  1. Click the plus (+) button at the point in the process where you want the script to run.

  2. Select Script from the available options.

  3. Click Generate and enter the following prompt:

  • "Add a custom script to sum all prices from the table list and calculate a total price."

This will generate a script step that performs the calculation and makes the result available for downstream steps in the process.

 

Once it is finished I can either accept or reject the proposed script. I can also amend the script which is necessary in some cases:

 

 

Summarize the process

In addition to the artifacts generated by the new GenAI feature, I’ve further refined the process—mapping the input and output values, and making additional adjustments to align with my specific requirements. As a result, we now have a completed and deployable process automation, ready to be made available to business users:

 

 

To ensure proper documentation for stakeholders like process owners, you can easily generate a summary of your entire process flow. Simply click the Generate button within the Process Builder, then select Explain this process. This will provide a clear, detailed overview of your process, making it easier for others to understand and review:

 

 

This will generate a detailed written documentation that you can easily share or upload to internal platforms such as your Wiki, or tools like Signavio or LeanIX. This ensures that the process is well-documented and accessible to all relevant stakeholders.

 

 

 

Some Tips and Tricks

When crafting a prompt, it’s important to be as precise as possible. Always use the verbs "Generate" or "Add", and reference the specific SAP Build Process Automation artifact names. Your prompt description should be between 5 and 1,000 characters in length.

If you're not satisfied with the result, you can either delete the artifact and generate a new one, or use the graphical layout designer to modify the artifact as needed. Additionally, beyond process steps, you can define process variables such as inputs, outputs, and custom variables to further refine your process.

If you run the generation to update an existing process, the following restrictions apply:

  • The process variables (input, output, custom) are not updated or removed.
  • Object-type inputs that refer to a data type are not supported.

Also make sure that you get familiar with all the known limitations on this feature, which are documented in our Help-Portal.

 

Happy hacking!

entry.title: Connect, Automate, and Thrive with 4 SAP BTP Use Cases You Cannot Miss at Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
Mark your calendars—SAP Sapphire 2025 is just around the corner (or you should already have the calendars marked and starting to pack). Sapphire 2025 will showcase SAP's strategic vision with SAP Business Suite, new innovations like Joule Agents, and industry-leading solutions in action. Whether you're seeking strategic insights or hands-on technology demonstrations, Sapphire 2025 promises to deliver value that extends well beyond the event itself.

 

SAP Business Technology Platform: The Backbone of Intelligent Enterprises

In this blog, I'll explore the breakthrough use cases for 2025 that demonstrate SAP's commitment to creating fully integrated business processes. We'll see how the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) serves as the essential foundation connecting disparate systems, processes, and data flows to deliver transformative business outcomes. Each use case is supported by comprehensive Discovery Center Missions and Business Accelerators designed to fast-track your implementation and maximize value creation.
For attendees joining us in person, our expert-led sessions will provide deep dives on how to get started with SAP BTP. Our specialists will also be available at the SAP BTP for Business Application booth to address your specific business challenges and help architect your connected enterprise vision. You don’t want to miss the following sessions in Orlando to learn more about the SAP BTP use cases and SAP Discovery Center, so add them to your agenda right now.


BTP1562 | Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP that you can implement today
BTP2467 | Mission possible: Accelerate transformation with SAP Discovery Center

 

New Business Technology Platform Use Cases: Unlocking End-to-End Connectivity 

In today's complex business environment, siloed operations and error-prone, fragmented processes create significant barriers to efficiency and growth. The true power of Intelligent Enterprise lies in seamless connectivity across your entire business landscape and utilizing the unified landscape to automate processes and apply insights analysis. Let's explore four new SAP BTP use cases for 2025 that will help your organization get started:

 

Use Case #1: Connect all applications across the business with SAP Integration Suite

• The Challenge: Organizations today operate with complex landscapes spanning cloud, on-premises, and hybrid environments. Critical business processes often span multiple applications, both from SAP and from other vendors. creating fragmentation that leads to inefficiencies and data inconsistencies. These fragmented processes add manual work, increase maintenance costs, and limit real-time access to valuable data.
• How SAP BTP Can Help: SAP Integration Suite provides a comprehensive integration platform that seamlessly connects your entire enterprise ecosystem—from SAP Business Suite applications to third-party solutions, legacy systems, and emerging technologies. The platform's pre-built integration content significantly accelerates implementation time, while its API management capabilities allow you to securely expose business processes to partners and developers.

By creating unified, end-to-end process flows, organizations can eliminate data silos and ensure consistent information across systems. Real-time data monitoring provides visibility into cross-system records, enabling proactive management of business operations. The outcome is a connected business environment where information flows across your enterprise landscape, driving efficiency. CONA Services is a great example of an organization that was able to minimize data errors and increase master data accuracy with SAP Integration Suite.

 

Use Case #2: Drive Your Business with Integrated Enterprise Performance Management

• The Challenge: Financial planning and performance management processes frequently operate in isolation from operational data sources. Finance teams spend excessive time collecting and reconciling data across systems rather than focusing on strategic analysis. This disconnect between financial planning and operational execution creates misalignment between corporate objectives and day-to-day business activities.
• How SAP BTP Can Help: SAP's integrated Enterprise Performance Management solution on BTP creates a seamless connection between financial planning and operational execution. By leveraging SAP BTP capabilities, organizations can assess new opportunities, improve profitability, and respond with greater agility in the face of unexpected change and disruption. It brings together data from Cloud ERP and other business applications to create a unified planning and performance management analysis with SAP Analytics Cloud.

This connected approach enables continuous planning cycles that adapt to changing business conditions. Operational data flows automatically into financial planning models, eliminating manual data collection and reconciliation efforts. Business leaders gain real-time visibility into how operational decisions impact financial outcomes, while predictive capabilities highlight future opportunities and risks. The result is truly connected planning where strategic objectives drive operational execution, and performance insights inform strategic adjustments in a continuous feedback loop. Freudenberg Home and Cleaning Solutions shares how they are aligning their operational planning with financial performance across multiple regions and business functions.

 

Use Case #3: Enhance Employee Onboarding Experience Through Streamlined Processes with AI

• The Challenge: The employee onboarding journey typically requires multiple people across multiple departments and systems—from HR and IT to facilities management and department-specific applications before the employee starts. From using document collection and antiquated paper-based verification to manual data errors, this fragmentation creates a poor experience for the new hire before they’ve even started and reduces productivity during the critical first months, while also increasing administrative burden across the organization. HR teams struggle to orchestrate complex cross-departmental workflows, while managers lack visibility into the onboarding progress.
• How SAP BTP Can Help: SAP BTP enables a revolutionary approach to employee onboarding by connecting previously isolated systems and processes into a unified experience. Through SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, organizations can create a single-entry point for all onboarding activities, while BTP's workflow management capabilities orchestrate tasks across systems and departments.

The solution connects SAP SuccessFactors with identity management, equipment provisioning, and department-specific processes and applications to create a seamless onboarding journey. New employees experience a coherent process regardless of the underlying systems, while HR teams and managers gain real-time visibility into onboarding progress and can proactively address potential delays. By connecting the entire onboarding ecosystem, organizations significantly reduce time-to-productivity for new hires while decreasing administrative overhead, ultimately transforming onboarding from a series of disconnected tasks to a strategic advantage in talent management. Gerdau S.A. demonstrates how this innovation not only improved the efficiency of the hiring process by leveraging the power of AI to reduce or eliminate manual data entry and review, but also improved the onboarding experience for both candidates and HR.

 

Use Case #4: Unlock Enterprise Automation with the Power of SAP BTP and SAP Signavio

• The Challenge: Business process automation initiatives often focus on isolated processes without considering end-to-end business outcomes. Organizations struggle to identify which processes should be prioritized for automation and how to ensure automated processes remain aligned with business objectives. After implementation, it's difficult to measure the actual impact of automation on business performance.
• How SAP BTP Can Help: The combination of SAP Signavio and SAP BTP creates a powerful solution for intelligent, end-to-end process automation. Signavio's process mining and business process management capabilities enable organizations to visualize current processes, identify bottlenecks, and simulate the impact of potential changes. This intelligence then guides automation implementation through BTP's comprehensive suite of automation tools, including SAP Build Process Automation and integration capabilities.

This integrated approach enables a continuous process improvement cycle: processes are analyzed, optimized, automated, and monitored in a connected loop. Organizations can prioritize automation initiatives based on business impact, implement them seamlessly across integrated systems, and measure the resulting outcomes accurately. By connecting process insights directly to automation implementation, businesses can achieve transformative results such as reduced cycle times, improved compliance, enhanced customer experiences, and significant cost savings while maintaining alignment with strategic objectives. Blue Diamond Growers’ prioritization of business values and elimination of process gaps with the insights from SAP Signavio allowed them to identify the most impactful automation opportunities.

 

“Must-Attend/Watch” SAP BTP Sessions

  • BTP2123 | Unleash the power of your applications, data, and AI with SAP BTP
    Orlando | Virtual
    Speaker: Michael Ameling
  • BTP2293 | Real stories, real impact: How customers are driving success with SAP BTP
    Orlando | Virtual
    Speaker: Bharat Sandhu
  • BTP2122: Explore the Roadmap for SAP Business Technology Platform
    Orlando | Virtual Speaker: Steffen Pietsch
  • BTP1562 | Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP that you can implement today
  • BTP2467 | Mission possible: Accelerate transformation with SAP Discovery Center
  • BTP2075 | Navigate the path to autonomous finance with SAP Business Suite and SAP BTP
  • BTP2072 | Discover how SAP customers are realizing business value with SAP BTP
  • BTP2074 | Maximize your move to SAP cloud ERP and SAP Business Suite with SAP BTP
  • BTP2468 | SAP BTP: The center of a clean core strategy for long-term success
  • BTP1591 | Unlock faster, scalable outcomes with SAP BTP as your foundation

 

To Wrap Up


As we look toward Sapphire 2025, it's clear that end-to-end connectivity is no longer just a technical consideration—it's a strategic imperative for business success. Whether you're focusing on enterprise integration, connected planning, streamlined employee experiences, or intelligent process automation, SAP Business Technology Platform stands as the essential foundation of SAP Business Suite. It connects systems, processes, and experiences across the enterprise landscape, enabling truly integrated business operations.
Visit the SAP Business Technology Platform In Action community page for comprehensive resources on these use cases and join us at Sapphire 2025 to experience firsthand how SAP BTP is redefining what's possible through end-to-end connectivity. Together, we'll explore how your organization can become truly connected, intelligent, and ready for whatever the future holds.
